2606.14459.title: MoDiCoL: A Modular Diagnostic Continual Learning Dataset for Robust Speech Recognition
2606.14459.paper_summary: Modern Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems have made remarkable progress on standard benchmarks, yet performance gaps have emerged under real-world distribution shifts, caused by recording conditions, accents, speech impairments, and noise. Existing datasets and benchmarks typically isolate these factors, which overlooks their co-occurrence in real-world applications. In this paper, we argue that model robustness can be treated as a dynamic capability that continually develops, and we introduce MoDiCoL, a Modular Diagnostic Continual Learning dataset designed for controlled analysis of linguistic content, speaker characteristics, and acoustic environments. Furthermore, we propose a real-world-inspired continual learning curriculum to simulate incremental updates and study how robustness is acquired, transferred, and forgotten. We evaluate three continual learning strategies and provide detailed insights into robustness under evolving conditions.
2606.14459.paper_summary_zh: 現代自動語音識別（ASR）系統在標準基準上取得了顯著進展，但在真實世界的分佈變化下，性能差距逐漸顯現，這些變化是由錄音條件、口音、語音障礙和噪音所引起的。現有的數據集和基準通常將這些因素孤立起來，忽視了它們在真實應用中的共存。在本文中，我們主張模型的穩健性可以被視為一種不斷發展的動態能力，並且我們介紹了MoDiCoL，一個模組化診斷持續學習數據集，旨在對語言內容、說話者特徵和聲學環境進行受控分析。此外，我們提出了一個受真實世界啟發的持續學習課程，以模擬增量更新並研究穩健性是如何獲得、轉移和遺忘的。我們評估了三種持續學習策略，並提供了對於在不斷變化的條件下穩健性的詳細見解。
2606.14459.author: Theresa Pekarek Rosin et.al.
2606.14459.authors: Theresa Pekarek Rosin, Matthias Kerzel, Stefan Wermter
